item.title: Drought deepens distress in Barind as yield falls, water crisis worsens
item.description: Climate change-induced drought has intensified across the Barind tract, pushing farmers into a cycle of falling crop production, rising costs and deepening livelihood insecurity, according to a recent study and field-level accounts....

item.title: Politician Amena Ahmed dies
item.description: Former Member of Parliament and executive president of a National Awami Party faction, Amena Ahmed, died in a city hospital on Saturday morning....

item.title: Rubaiyat Jamil dies
item.description: Founder and chief executive officer of ICE Technologies, Rubaiyat Jamil, died on Friday at the age of 46, said a statement issued by the Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry on Saturday....Geographic Reference

item.title: BMU begins MD course in emergency medicine
item.description: The MD course in emergency medicine has started at the Bangladesh Medical University....

item.title: Schools, colleges, madrassahs to resume classes today
item.description: Schools, colleges and madrassahs across the country are scheduled to resume academic activities today following the combined holiday for Ramadan, Eid-ul-Fitr and several other occasions for over a month....

item.title: Dhaka-bound passengers crowd Aricha, Paturia
item.description: Throngs of passengers were seen crowding the Aricha and Paturia ferry terminals in Manikganj on Saturday on their return journey to Dhaka at the end of the Eid-ul-Fitr holiday....

item.title: Govt mulls restoring old police uniform: minister
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Saturday said that the government would soon decide on restoring one of the earlier uniforms previously used by the police, as members of the force are dissatisfied with the current uniform....

item.title: Meeting today for improved road safety, traffic management
item.description: The road transport and bridges ministry is going to hold a high-level meeting today to determine immediate action to prevent road crashes, enhance road safety and improve traffic management....

item.title: Tug of war over judicial control
item.description: The Supreme Court Secretariat Ordinance, 2025, designed to grant financial and administrative autonomy to the judiciary, has become a politically sensitive issue as a parliamentary committee is reviewing its provisions, particularly those related to the......

item.title: Tug of war over judicial control
item.description: The Supreme Court Secretariat Ordinance, 2025, designed to grant financial and administrative autonomy to the judiciary, has become a politically sensitive issue as a parliamentary committee is reviewing its provisions, particularly those related to the......

item.title: BB injects Tk 68,245cr into 12 weak banks
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has so far injected about Tk 68,245 crore into 12 crisis-hit banks under a special liquidity arrangement, effectively creating new money to keep the lenders afloat......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: RMG exports to EU surge 36pc in 5 years
item.description: The country’s readymade garment exports to the European Union witnessed a growth of 35.82 per cent between 2021 and 2025, reaching 19.41 billion euros, bolstering its position as Europe’s second-largest supplier......

item.title: UNDP campaign removes over 1.1t of waste from Cox’s Bazar
item.description: A three-day beach clean-up and awareness campaign led by UNDP Bangladesh under the Plastics Circularity Project removed 173 bags and sacks of waste weighing over 1,168 kilograms from Cox’s Bazar sea beach....

item.title: Bank Asia to acquire Bank Alfalah stake for $47.5m
item.description: The shareholders of Pakistan-based Bank Alfalah Limited have approved the sale and transfer of its Bangladesh operations to Dhaka-based Bank Asia Limited for approximately $47.5 million (equivalent to around Tk 580 crore)......

item.title: Fruit prices decline sharply after Ramadan as demand drops
item.description: Fruit prices in Bangladesh have decreased significantly after Ramadan and Eid, easing the financial burden on consumers following the earlier surge in costs......

item.title: Vietnam to import potato from Bangladesh
item.description: The government of Vietnam has officially opened its market for the import of potatoes from Bangladesh......

item.title: Sonamasjid Land Port resumes activities
item.description: Import and export activities at Sonamasjid Land Port in Chapainawabganj have resumed following a 10-day suspension due to the holidays marking Eid-ul-Fitr and Independence Day......

item.title: Israeli forces shoot dead Palestinian teenager
item.description: The Palestinian health ministry said Saturday that Israeli forces shot dead a teenager in the occupied West Bank, while the military said troops had fired in response to a threat....

item.title: Strikes across ME on, war enters 2nd month
item.description: Gulf countries and Israel came under missile fire and Israeli forces struck Iran on Saturday, as the war raged into its second month with Washington expressing hopes for progress in talks with Tehran....

item.title: Anti-Trump protests launch on ‘No Kings’ day in US
item.description: Massive protests against president Donald Trump kicked off Saturday across the United States and beyond, as millions of people vent fury over what they see as his authoritarian bent and other forms of cruel, law-trampling governance....

item.title: Khagrachhari farmers thrive as papaya brings change
item.description: A young farmer, Remrachai Marma, has turned the tide for his village in the district by achieving success in papaya cultivation, inspiring a wave of agricultural transformation in his community......

item.title: FM likely to visit New Delhi on April 7
item.description: Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman is likely to pay his maiden official visit to India on April 7 and hold bilateral talks with his Indian counterpart S Jaishankar in New Delhi....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Solar Energy may turn SMEs into growth hubs
item.description: SMEs in BSCIC industrial estates could cut more than 14.09 million tonnes of carbon dioxide emissions, earn up to $0.40 million annually through carbon credits, and reduce operational costs by 30 to 50 per cent by adopting decentralised rooftop solar energy, a new study has found......

item.title: Maersk stops Oman operations after drone attack
item.description: Danish shipping giant Maersk said operations had been temporarily halted at Oman’s port of Salalah after a drone attack on Saturday injured one worker and damaged a crane......

item.title: BNP PP meeting discusses fuel hoarding, Eid journey accidents
item.description: The BNP parliamentary party meeting on Saturday discussed issues such as preventing fuel oils hoarding and smuggling, accidents in roads, railways and waterways during the Eid journey, and the decline in international aid for the Rohingya....

item.title: Italy probes cosmetic sales to minors
item.description: Italian competition regulators said on Friday they have opened a probe into French luxury group LVMH and its Sephora and Benefit subsidiaries for promoting skin care products to minors......

item.title: IMF to unlock $1.2b for Pakistan
item.description: The International Monetary Fund announced on Friday that it has reached a staff-level agreement with Pakistan to unlock a new $1.2 billion package as part of its support programs for the country......

item.title: 22 migrants die in sea
item.description: At least 26 migrants, including 21 Bangladeshis, were rescued from the Mediterranean Sea after 22 other migrants died during a six-day ordeal at sea off the Greek coast while attempting to reach Europe from Libya, reports agencies....

item.title: Egypt imposes business curfew to counter fuel costs
item.description: Egypt has ordered shops, restaurants and shopping malls to close from 9:00 pm from Saturday, hoping to curb energy bills that have more than doubled because of the Iran war......

item.title: 35 children die since January as measles surges
item.description: At least 35 children have died of measles and several hundred others have been infected across the country since January this year, raising alarm among public health experts....

item.title: UN task force to aid passage of fertiliser through Hormuz
item.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has launched an interagency task force to help secure the passage of fertilisers through the Strait of Hormuz, his spokesperson announced on Friday......

item.title: War leaves 6,000 tonnes of tea stuck in Kenya
item.description: Between 6,000 and 8,000 tonnes of tea, worth around $24 million, is stuck at Kenya’s port of Mombasa because of the war in the Middle East, trade officials said Friday......

item.title: AI looks to gas to power its rapid expansion
item.description: Natural gas took center stage this week at the world’s largest energy conference, as major market players discussed how to power the rise of artificial intelligence......

item.title: Thailand reaches deal for vessels to transit Hormuz
item.description: Thailand has reached an agreement with Iran to allow Thai oil vessels safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, the Southeast Asian nation’s prime minister said on Saturday......

item.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela minerals
item.description: The US Treasury Department said Friday that it had lifted more sanctions against Venezuela’s gold and other mineral industries, as it pushes for greater access to the South American country’s vast natural resources......

item.title: US court overturns $16b penalty against Argentina
item.description: A US appeals court on Friday overturned a $16.1 billion judgment against Argentina for nationalising the oil company YPF in 2012......

item.title: Oil climbs, stocks fall as markets see no end to war
item.description: Oil prices rose and stocks fell Friday as the United States and Israel struck Iranian nuclear sites, denting optimism over potential talks to end nearly a month of war in the Middle East......

item.title: Precision farming for a warming Bangladesh
item.description: PRECISION agriculture stands out as one of the most effective dual-purpose strategies for both adapting to and mitigating climate change in Bangladesh. In a country ranked among the most climate-vulnerable in the world, where rising temperatures, erratic rainfall, increasing salinity, frequent floods and cyclones already jeopardise food security for over 170 million......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Re-imagining school access, equity
item.description: THE government’s decision to abolish the lottery-based school admission system and re-introduce admission tests from the 2027 academic year has re-ignited an important national debate about fairness, merit and access. At one level, the move appears to respond to a widely shared concern that random selection cannot adequately assess a child’s readiness for......

item.title: The overlooked foundation
item.description: THE constitution of Bangladesh recognises shelter as one of the basic necessities of life, alongside food, clothing, education and medical care. Over the decades, successive governments have made substantial investments in food security, primary education, health care and social protection. These investments have helped improve literacy, reduce poverty and raise life......

item.title: Ecology of canal revival
item.description: WATER is life. Yet across continents, this limited vital resource is vanishing at an alarming rate. Only about 2.5 per cent of Earth’s water is freshwater, and most of that is locked in glaciers, ice caps, or deep groundwater. Accessible freshwater (rivers, lakes, and shallow groundwater) is a very small fraction. Key sources are surface water (rivers, lakes, and wetlands);......

item.title: Health sector drives may fail without policy support
item.description: PRIVATE sector healthcare facilities have largely been unregulated, leading to medical malpractice. In this context, recent drive against errant health facilities is welcome. In the drive beginning on March 23, the Directorate General of Health Services has closed down 13 hospitals for major violations, including operating facilities with unqualified staff, substandard......

item.title: Govt must fix distribution failures in fuel crisis
item.description: LONG queues of vehicles tailing from filling stations, waiting for several hours along the road, have now become commonplace, whilst many filling stations stop selling fuel early or altogether, citing a supply shortage. Whilst this goes on, the government keeps saying that there is no supply shortage of fuel. The shortage episode mostly involves petrol, diesel and......

item.title: Woman dies from electrocution
item.description: A woman, Kajoli Begum, 50, was killed after being electrocuted by a snapped power line during a storm in Rangpur....

item.title: 8 pre-inspection teams to monitor SSC exams centres
item.description: Dhaka secondary and higher secondary education board has taken extensive preparations to conduct this year’s Secondary School Certificate examinations smoothly and in an environment free from cheating....Maps

item.title: Nine BAF personnel awarded honourary commissions
item.description: On the occasion of Independence and National Day 2026, the Bangladesh Air Force conferred honorary commissions on nine personnel, recognising their long service, professionalism and contributions....

item.title: DNCC launches anti-dengue drive in 536 schools
item.description: Dhaka North City Corporation on Saturday launched a special cleanliness and mosquito extermination campaign across 536 educational institutions to prevent dengue infections among students....

item.title: Mentally unstable man found dead in Barishal river
item.description: The body of a mentally unstable man who had been reported missing was recovered from the Korai River near Rajarchar in Barishal Sadar Saturday morning....

item.title: Fakhrul for integrated plan to expedite dev works
item.description: Local government, rural development and co-operatives minister and BNP secretary general Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Saturday underscored the need for an integrated plan to accelerate development activities of the local government engineering department....Political commentary Bangladesh

item.title: Three arrested as man killed by ‘friends’ for auto-rickshaw
item.description: A man, whose headless body was recovered from the Kaliganga River in Manikganj, was allegedly killed by his friends who wanted to steal his battery-run auto-rickshaw, said the police....

item.title: 3 killed in lightning strikes in 2 dists
item.description: At least three people were killed and four others injured in separate lighting strikes in Jhenaidah and Magura districts on Saturday....

item.title: CUET’s PME department ranks in QS subject ranking
item.description: The department of petroleum and mining engineering of Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology has ranked for the fourth time in the QS World University Rankings in its subject ranking....

item.title: Section 144 imposed in Habiganj
item.description: Local administration imposed Section 144 of the Code of Criminal Procedure restricting public gathering in an area of Madhabpur upazila in Habiganj on Friday afternoon as two groups announced separate waz mahfil (prayer sessions) at the same venue at the same time....

item.title: Russian strikes kill 4 in Ukraine, damage port, maternity hospital
item.description: Russian air attacks across Ukraine early Saturday killed at least four people and damaged critical infrastructure, including a port and a maternity hospital, authorities said, as Russia pressed on with its war against Ukraine....

item.title: Search continues for fourth day after bus plunges into Padma
item.description: Diving teams from the Fire Service and Civil Defence and the Bangladesh Navy continued search and rescue operations for the fourth consecutive day on Saturday after a bus plunged into the Padma River at Daulatdia ferry jetty under Goalanda upazila in Rajbari district....

item.title: 1 killed, 15 injured in Sunamganj village clash
item.description: A man was killed and 15 others were injured in a clash between two groups of villagers at Doarabazar upazila in Sunamganj on Friday night over cutting paddy saplings....

item.title: Army defuses 7 landmines in Chuadanga farmland
item.description: A Bangladesh army bomb disposal team defused seven landmines discovered in an agricultural field in Makhaldanga Schoolpara area of Chuadanga Sadar upazila on Saturday....

item.title: Govt seeks permanent fix for Bhabadah water stagnation
item.description: State minister for power, energy and mineral resources Aninda Islam Amit on Saturday said that the incumbent government was looking for a permanent solution to Bhabadah waterlogging in Jashore....

item.title: Doctors urged to back health sector advancement
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain on Saturday called for greater cooperation from physicians to advance the country’s medical sector, saying coordinated efforts driven by honesty and dedication are essential to achieve desired goals....

item.title: Man hacked to death in Kushtia
item.description: A 38-year-old man was allegedly hacked to death by his rivals over a previous enmity in Kumarkhali upazila of Kushtia on Friday night....

item.title: Reserved JS seat, minister for plain land national minorities demanded
item.description: Leaders of ethnic minority communities on Saturday urged the government to appoint a technocrat minister from plains ethnic groups and allocate a reserved parliamentary seat for an ethnic minority woman....

item.title: Three more held for killing RAB member at Jungle Salimpur
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion said on Saturday that they had arrested three more accused in the case of the January 19 killing of the battalion member Abdul Motaleb in Chattogram’s Jungle Salimpur area....

item.title: Transformation of ordinance into law demanded
item.description: Leaders of the Bangladesh Trade Union Federation on Saturday urged the parliament to enact the Bangladesh labour act amendment ordinance, 2025 in the next session for the welfare of the workers....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: BARI celebrates Independence, National Day
item.description: The Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute has celebrated the Independence and National Day in Gazipur....

item.title: 5 injured as storm ravages Lalmonirhat villages
item.description: Several villages in Lalmonirhat were devastated on Friday night by storms and hailstorms, causing damage to homesteads and standing crops and five people were injured as their houses were hit by hailstones, said Lalmonirhat deputy commissioner Rakib Haider....

item.title: Discussion on Nazrul’s ghazals held at Chhayanaut
item.description: A discussion on national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam’s ghazals was held on Saturday at an event titled Bhasa Sanskritir Alap, organised by Chhayanaut in their seminar room at Dhanmondi, Dhaka....

item.title: Udichi observes birth anniversary of Satyen Sen
item.description: Bangladesh Udichi Shilpigosthi observed the 119th birth anniversary of its founder, Satyen Sen, at Topkhana Road in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: Novera Ahmed birth anniv today
item.description: Today is the 87th anniversary of birth of sculptor Novera Ahmed, who is considered the first modern sculptor in the country....

item.title: Folk festival marks Shah Abdul Karim’s 110th birth anniv
item.description: A two-day folk festival has commenced in Sunamganj to celebrate the 110th birth anniversary of Ekushey Padak-winning Baul legend Shah Abdul Karim....
